title: Who won the 2025-26 Serie A (Italian top-flight football)?
rules: If Napoli wins the Serie A, then the market resolves to Yes.
tavily_brief: [redacted] Let me compile this. [redacted] With four to five matches remaining, Inter's mathematical path to the Scudetto is nearly certain. Inter need just four points from their final five games to secure the title, and face Parma next weekend—a team with minimal remaining objectives. A victory would mathematically clinch Inter's 21st league title regardless of Napoli's results (Yahoo Sports, April 26). Napoli's title defense has faltered significantly from pre-season expectations. While the reigning champions began 2025-26 as betting favorites at +150 to +160 odds (OneFootball, Sports Interaction), they have been unable to maintain pace with Inter under new coach Cristian Chivu. Napoli did win the Supercoppa Italiana in December 2025 and [redacted] The addition of Kevin De Bruyne and Rasmus Højlund provided attacking depth, yet the Champions League's additional fixture burden—absent from Napoli's 2024-25 title run—has proven costly. Napoli sit 11 points clear of fifth place (Como and Roma), securing Champions League qualification, but the Scudetto is mathematically beyond reach barring an unprecedented collapse by Inter. Inter's consistency under Chivu, despite early skepticism about the inexperienced manager, has been decisive. Even after drawing 2-2 at Torino on April 26, Inter maintained their 10-point cushion. AC Milan (67 points) and Juventus (64 points) remain in contention for top-four finishes but are too far behind to threaten the title. Historical context: none of the last five Serie A defending champions retained the title the following season, making Napoli's position unsurprising (Sports Mole, August 2025). **Most important facts:** (1) Inter holds a 10-point lead with four matches remaining and need only four points to mathematically clinch; (2) Napoli's title defense has been undermined by Champions League demands and inconsistency, leaving them 10 points adrift with minimal mathematical hope; (3) Inter face Parma next—a team with nothing to play for—making a title-clinching victory highly probable within days.

iterations.content: So as of Dec 7, 2025, Napoli beat Juventus 2-1. Let me get the current standings picture - it's a tight race between Napoli, Milan, Inter, Roma.

  [pre-cutoff 2025-05-18] Serie A Match Scores — May 18, 2025
  URL: https://www.espn.com/serie-a/scoreboard/_/date/20250518?origin=exa&t=6a09f91640f339e4ae7533bb
  Serie A results for May 18, 2025. 1 match: AC Milan vs AS Roma. | Match | Status | Score / Time | Venue | |:------|:-------|:-------------|:------| | AC Milan 1 - AS Roma 3 | Final | AC Milan 1 - AS Roma 3 (W) | Stadio Olimpico, Rome |

SYNTHESIZED SUMMARY (debiased, pre-cutoff sources only):
Inter Milan placed 2nd with 74 points and Napoli 3rd with 72 points.** --- **Note:** The search results included match scores from December 29, 2024 (Roma 1-1 AC Milan) and May 18, 2025 (AC Milan 1-3 Roma), but these were scoreboard entries without publication dates that could be verified as strictly before April 30, 2026. The primary substantive source above provides the clearest factual summary of Serie A standings involving Milan and Roma during the 2025 period.

args.reasoning:
Evidence inventory:
- Dec 7, 2025: Napoli beat Juventus 2-1 (ESPN match report confirms).
- Nov 30, 2025: "Napoli beat rivals Roma to join Milan at Serie A summit" — Napoli on 28 points, level with Milan on goal difference, just 1 point ahead of Roma and Inter. Conte's Napoli described as in a "tight Scudetto battle."
- Oct 25, 2025: Napoli beat Inter 3-1 to return to top of Serie A. Inter recently lost to Torino 1-0 and were humbled 6-2 by PSV in Champions League.
- Dec 6, 2025: Inter beat Como 4-0 (strong recent form).
- Last season (2024-25): Napoli won the title, Inter finished 2nd with 74, Napoli 3rd... actually Napoli won. The race is genuinely four-way: Napoli, Milan, Roma, Inter all within 1-2 points in early December 2025.

Implication: Kalshi prices Inter at 0.99, which is wildly inconsistent with the actual standings showing a 4-way tight race with Napoli/Milan leading on points. Inter is not even top of the table currently. This appears to be a Kalshi data error or stale pricing — the true distribution should be spread across Napoli, Milan, Inter, Roma roughly.

However, I must respect that Kalshi's market may reflect information I'm missing, and Inter has elite squad depth. But evidence-based: with ~14 matches played out of 38 and four teams within 1 point, no team should be near 99%. Reasonable distribution: Napoli ~30% (current co-leader, defending champs, Conte), Inter ~28% (squad quality, recent form), Milan ~22% (co-leader with Napoli), Roma ~12% (in mix but historically less title-experienced), then long tail for Bologna/Juventus/Atalanta/others.

Upset check: Cremonese beat Bologna 3-1, but they're not contenders. The strong signal is that Inter is NOT clearly the favorite — Napoli has direct H2H win over Inter and is leading. This justifies major deviation from market.

Indeed, Napoli cannot reach that figure even if they win each of their six remaini

  [pre-cutoff 2026-04-17] Inter Milan eases past Cagliari and moves one step closer to Serie A title | FOX Sports
  URL: https://www.foxsports.com/articles/soccer/inter-milan-eases-past-cagliari-and-moves-one-step-closer-to-serie-a-title
  Inter Milan eases past Cagliari and moves one step closer to Serie A title | FOX Sports [...] # Inter Milan eases past Cagliari and moves one step closer to Serie A title [...] Published Apr. 17, 2026 5:48 p.m. [...] The result put the champions-elect 12 points clear of Napoli with six games remaining. [...] Cagliari was in 16th place, six points above the relegation zone. [...] Como’s first away 

SYNTHESIZED SUMMARY (debiased, pre-cutoff sources only):

args.reasoning: Evidence inventory and implications: 1) Strong — Fox Sports (Apr 17, 2026) reported Inter “eases past Cagliari” and moves “one step closer” to the title, explicitly stating Inter were 12 points clear of Napoli with six matches remaining. Evidence shows a double‑digit lead with only six fixtures left; therefore, only an unprecedented collapse plus a perfect rival run could flip the title. 2) Strong — SempreInter (Apr 14, 2026), citing Gazzetta dello Sport/FCInterNews, detailed the mathematical clinch path, noting Inter could seal the Scudetto in early May and that “85 is the magic number,” with Napoli unable to reach that figure even by winning all six remaining matches. Evidence shows Inter’s points target was already beyond Napoli’s maximum; therefore, the title race was functionally decided before May. 3) Moderate — Multiple outlets labeled Inter “champions‑elect” by mid‑April with six to play, reflecting convergent standings arithmetic; no contemporaneous reports flagged sanctions/points deductions that could erase a 12‑point cushion. Evidence shows no exogenous risk (penalties or forfeits) threatening the lead; therefore, reversal paths are even narrower than form‑based scenarios. Upset check: No strong contrary signal (e.g., major points deduction, mass injuries, managerial sacking before season end). Simone Inzaghi’s future links emerged post‑season and do not affect 2025‑26 resolution. Sanity/base rate: Serie A leaders with a 12‑point advantage at six‑to‑go almost never fail to win the title absent extraordinary events. Synthesis: The standings arithmetic and clinch scenarios identified in mid‑April 2026 overwhelmingly imply Inter finished as 2025‑26 champions. I assign a dominant probability to Inter and minimal residual probability mass to all other listed clubs given the lack of any specific, late‑breaking reversal signal.

agent_results.reasoning: Evidence strongly favors Inter: 10-point lead with 4 matches remaining, needs only 4 points to clinch, facing Parma next. Napoli mathematically nearly eliminated, 10 points adrift. This is a STRONG signal favoring Inter (already at 0.99 market). Napoli has no path. Staying near market prices.

Question asks who wins 2025-26 Serie A. Evidence is overwhelmingly clear: Inter leads by 10 points with 4 matches remaining, needs only 4 points to clinch mathematically, faces Parma (no objectives) next. Napoli is 10 points adrift with minimal mathematical hope. Milan and Juventus too far back. Kalshi snapshot has Inter at 0.99, all others at 0.005-0.010. STRONG signals all point to Inter. A 10-p

P1: Evidence is decisive — Inter leads by 10 points with 4 matches remaining and needs only 4 points to mathematically clinch. Napoli is 10 points adrift with minimal mathematical hope. This implies Napoli probability is near zero (~0.01-0.02).
P2: Base rate for a defending champion 10 points back with 4 games left to overturn the deficit is extremely low historically; none of the last five Serie

Evidence states Inter holds a 10-point lead with four matches remaining and needs only 4 points to mathematically clinch, while Napoli is 10 points adrift with minimal mathematical hope. Inter faces Parma next, a team with nothing to play for. Napoli's title defense has faltered under Chivu and Champions League fixture burden. The market reflects this at Inter @ 0.99.

Evidence inventory and implications: 1) Strong — Fox Sports (Apr 17, 2026) reported Inter “eases past Cagliari” and moves “one step closer” to the title, explicitly stating Inter were 12 points clear of Napoli with six matches remaining. Evidence shows a double‑digit lead with only six fixtures left; therefore, only an unprecedented collapse plus a perfect rival run could flip the title. 2) Strong — SempreInter (Apr 14, 2026), citing Gazzetta dello Sport/FCInterNews, detailed the mathematical clinch path, noting Inter could seal the Scudetto in early May and that “85 is the magic number,” with Napoli unable to reach that figure even by winning all six remaining matches. Evidence shows Inter’s points target was already beyond Napoli’s maximum; therefore, the title race was functionally decided before May. 3) Moderate — Multiple outlets labeled Inter “champions‑elect” by mid‑April with six to play, reflecting convergent standings arithmetic; no contemporaneous reports flagged sanctions/points deductions that could erase a 12‑point cushion. Evidence shows no exogenous risk (penalties or forfeits) threatening the lead; therefore, reversal paths are even narrower than form‑based scenarios. Upset check: No strong contrary signal (e.g., major points deduction, mass injuries, managerial sacking before season end). Simone Inzaghi’s future links emerged post‑season and do not affect 2025‑26 resolution. Sanity/base rate: Serie A leaders with a 12‑point advantage at six‑to‑go almost never fail to win the title absent extraordinary events. Synthesis: The standings arithmetic and clinch scenarios identified in mid‑April 2026 overwhelmingly imply Inter finished as 2025‑26 champions. I assign a dominant probability to Inter and minimal residual probability mass to all other listed clubs given the lack of any specific, late‑breaking reversal signal.

Evidence inventory:
- Dec 7, 2025: Napoli beat Juventus 2-1 (ESPN match report confirms).
- Nov 30, 2025: "Napoli beat rivals Roma to join Milan at Serie A summit" — Napoli on 28 points, level with Milan on goal difference, just 1 point ahead of Roma and Inter. Conte's Napoli described as in a "tight Scudetto battle."
- Oct 25, 2025: Napoli beat Inter 3-1 to return to top of Serie A. Inter recently lost to Torino 1-0 and were humbled 6-2 by PSV in Champions League.
- Dec 6, 2025: Inter beat Como 4-0 (strong recent form).
- Last season (2024-25): Napoli won the title, Inter finished 2nd with 74, Napoli 3rd... actually Napoli won. The race is genuinely four-way: Napoli, Milan, Roma, Inter all within 1-2 points in early December 2025.

Implication: Kalshi prices Inter at 0.99, which is wildly inconsistent with the actual standings showing a 4-way tight race with Napoli/Milan leading on points. Inter is not even top of the table currently. This appears to be a Kalshi data error or stale pricing — the true distribution should be spread across Napoli, Milan, Inter, Roma roughly.

However, I must respect that Kalshi's market may reflect information I'm missing, and Inter has elite squad depth. But evidence-based: with ~14 matches played out of 38 and four teams within 1 point, no team should be near 99%. Reasonable distribution: Napoli ~30% (current co-leader, defending champs, Conte), Inter ~28% (squad quality, recent form), Milan ~22% (co-leader with Napoli), Roma ~12% (in mix but historically less title-experienced), then long tail for Bologna/Juventus/Atalanta/others.

Upset check: Cremonese beat Bologna 3-1, but they're not contenders. The strong signal is that Inter is NOT clearly the favorite — Napoli has direct H2H win over Inter and is leading. This justifies major deviation from market.

iterations.content:
The evidence is strongly aligned: Inter holds a ~10-12 point lead with ~4-6 matches remaining, needs just 4 points to mathematically clinch, faces Parma next. The Kalshi market has Inter at 0.990. Four of five agents (NARRATIVE, COT, TOT, CALIBRATED Opus) cluster Inter at 0.91-0.97. DEEP_1 (gpt-5) is at 0.62 (likely over-cautious on small residual risk). DEEP_0 appears to have anchored on outdated November/December 2025 evidence when Napoli was tied with Milan — this is stale information and should be discounted heavily.

Per AIA rules: 4/5 agents converge near market (~0.95+); one outlier (DEEP_0) is clearly using stale evidence. My search confirms Inter's dominance through April 2026 (Inter beat Bologna 3-1 in January, beat Como 4-0 in December, drew Torino 2-2 on April 26). The April 26 draw still leaves Inter on path with ~10-pt lead.

I'll place Inter near market (0.96), slightly below the 0.99 Kalshi price to retain some uncertainty about the final mathematical clinch. Distribute residual probability primarily to Napoli/Milan/Roma/Bologna as the next-best teams.

args.reasoning:
KEY DISAGREEMENT: 4/5 agents (all Opus variants) cluster Inter at 0.91-0.97 matching market (0.99). Two outliers: DEEP_1 at 0.62 (over-cautious) and DEEP_0 at 0.18 (anchored on stale Nov/Dec 2025 evidence when Napoli was tied at the top). 

CLASSIFICATION: FACTUAL — disagreement stems from which evidence is most recent. Some agents weighted November snapshots; others weighted late-April clinch evidence.

RESOLUTION via search: Confirmed Inter beat Bologna 3-1 (Jan 4), beat Como 4-0 (Dec 6), drew Torino 2-2 (Apr 26). The Apr 26 evidence ("one step closer", "needs 4 points from 5 games", "12 clear with 6 to go") is the most recent and decisive. DEEP_0's Nov 30 evidence is clearly stale and superseded. DEEP_1's 0.62 underweights mathematical near-certainty.

Per AIA: resist outlier pull when outliers are demonstrably using older info. The 4-agent consensus near 0.95 aligns with market (0.99) and base rates for a 10-pt lead with 4 games left (historically >95% conversion). I set Inter at 0.95, slightly below market for residual risk. Residual mass to Napoli/Milan/Roma/Bologna as nearest chasers.
